name: Low Bot Score Login Challenge
objective: Challenge low-confidence browser traffic targeting login and sign-in flows to reduce credential stuffing and scripted recon before hard blocking.
risk_mitigated: Automated login abuse, account enumeration, and low-volume bot reconnaissance against authentication surfaces.
potential_side_effects: Legacy clients, embedded browsers, and automation that cannot complete JavaScript challenges will fail if the rule is applied too broadly.
tuning_notes: Apply only to browser-based login flows, not API token endpoints. Raise the bot-score threshold gradually and keep mobile SDK endpoints out of scope unless they can satisfy the challenge flow.
deployment_notes: Start with log-only analytics around bot scores on login paths, then move to JS challenge once you confirm that browser clients dominate the targeted routes.
monitoring_notes: Track challenge solve rate, repeated challenged IPs, and login failure spikes after rollout. Compare solve rates by ASN and country to identify automated pressure.
cloudflare_expression: ((http.request.uri.path contains "/login" or http.request.uri.path contains "/sign-in" or http.request.uri.path contains "/auth/login") and cf.bot_management.score lt 25 and http.request.method in {"GET" "POST"})
log_only_expression: ((http.request.uri.path contains "/login" or http.request.uri.path contains "/sign-in" or http.request.uri.path contains "/auth/login") and cf.bot_management.score lt 25 and http.request.method in {"GET" "POST"})
